title: phacelophrynium nicobaricum
intro: Phacelophrynium nicobaricum, commonly known as the Nicobar lanceolate, is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae, native to the Nicobar Islands in the Bay of Bengal. It is traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Phacelophrynium nicobaricum include wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and extracts, and are often used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
